articles.headline: Anthropic settles authors' class action for $1.5B, clearing path for broader AI training data use
articles.summary: A federal judge in San Francisco approved Anthropic's $1.5 billion settlement with authors who claimed their books were misused to train Claude, a landmark resolution that reduces legal risk for AI firms and may accelerate commercial adoption of generative AI across enterprises [1].

articles.headline: Nvidia releases Cosmos 3 Edge, a local-run physical AI model enabling robots to operate without data centers
articles.summary: Nvidia launched Cosmos 3 Edge, a 4B-parameter open-source AI model that runs locally on devices, allowing robots to understand their environment without cloud dependency—reducing latency, cutting cloud costs, and enhancing operational efficiency for automation deployments [1][9].

articles.headline: U.S. Business AI Adoption Tops 20% as Nearly a Quarter of Firms Plan Integration Within Six Months
articles.summary: Goldman Sachs Global Investment Research reports that over 20% of U.S. businesses now integrate AI into operations, with nearly 25% of firms expecting to adopt the technology within the next six months, signaling rapid operational scaling for SMEs and corporations.
